quote:
When the black tide slackens, even the most fertile lands question the worth of what feeds them.

A silent pause between armies invites a secret garden where children plot with tomatoes instead of barricades.

When a star turns down the stage, the applause of the wind tells a different story.

When faith gathers like clouds over a lake, the world sees a single drop turning rivers of hope.

A notorious hand found in foreign sands shows that even deserts remember the footprints of those who cross them.

When a man brandishes a weapon in a judge's hall, the law remains a blade sheathed, not a threat.

When a mind made of code becomes too wise, it forgets the human touch that painted its first stroke.
quote_preview: When the black tide slackens, even the most fertile lands question the worth of what feeds them.
